rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.reason: Wikipedia biology source about Welwitschia; not an LLM architecture paper and contains no full content-word phrases.
Cumulative LoRA reuses frozen past adapters and adds a new trainable adapter summed during inference — functionally identical to WMCL's append-only single-edge growth with strip-stack inference.
C-LoRA continual low-rank adaptation manages frozen past plus new trainable adapters with routing/orthogonality — same append-with-frozen-past family as WMCL.
Shared LoRA subspaces for strict continual learning operate as a single-edge continually-updated low-rank substrate — adjacent strip-stack mechanism to WMCL.
HAM dynamically merges accumulated adapters as tasks arrive — same accumulation-of-frozen-strips primitive with merging instead of distance-decay convolution.
Online-LoRA performs task-free online continual LoRA, appending new adapters in a stream — the same one-edge-append continual mechanism as WMCL.
STABLE uses gating logic rather than pure append-only single-edge growth; below all three thresholds.
Survey of continual learning methods, not a specific append-only single-growth-edge mechanism; below all thresholds.

fail_reason: 5 prior works hit the functional and/or semantic thresholds: rank 2 (Cumulative LoRA, func 0.92, sem 0.84) is the strongest near-duplicate operationally matching WMCL's frozen-past + new-trainable strip-stack inference, plus ranks 3-6 cover continual-LoRA routing, shared-subspace, hierarchical merging, and online-LoRA append-streams — the append-only-with-frozen-past adapter family is densely populated, so WMCL is not novel.
